metadata.description: Fill the 5 biostatistics flow gaps needed for factorial ANOVA analysis: two-way ANOVA (eta-squared/omega-squared), calibration metrics suite, Decision Curve Analysis, Riley bootstrap instability, and interaction plots. Also adds cost appendix table generation and TRIPOD compliance verification.

references.ref: Lakens (2013). "Calculating and reporting effect sizes." Frontiers in Psychology.
Van Calster et al. (2016). "A calibration hierarchy for risk models." Stat Med.
Riley et al. (2021). "Minimum sample size for developing a multivariable prediction model." Stat Med.
Maier-Hein et al. (2024). "Metrics Reloaded." Nature Methods.

metadata.existing_infrastructure: biostatistics_flow.py: 9 tasks, fully orchestrated biostatistics_statistics.py: pairwise, Bayesian, Friedman+ICC biostatistics_figures.py: 3 figures (effect size heatmap, forest plot, distribution) biostatistics_tables.py: 4 tables (main comparison, effect size, variance, ranking) biostatistics_rankings.py: multi-metric ranking + Nemenyi CD

phase.name: Two-Way Factorial ANOVA
phase.description: The core statistical gap. Implements Model x Loss two-way ANOVA with eta-squared (partial) and omega-squared (bias-corrected) effect sizes. Uses pingouin for primary analysis, statsmodels for supplementary interaction.

task.name: Two-way ANOVA with effect sizes
task.description:
Add compute_factorial_anova() to biostatistics_statistics.py.
Input: per_volume_data dict with structure: {metric_name: {condition_key: {fold_id: np.ndarray}}} where condition_key encodes model and loss (e.g., "dynunet__dice_ce")
The function must: 1. Parse condition_key into model and loss factors 2. Build a long-format DataFrame (columns: volume_id, model, loss, fold, metric_value) 3. Run pingouin.anova() for two-way ANOVA with Model, Loss, Model:Loss interaction 4. Extract partial eta-squared from pingouin output 5. Compute omega-squared (bias-corrected) per Lakens (2013): omega_sq = (SS_effect - df_effect * MS_error) / (SS_total + MS_error) 6. Run statsmodels Type III ANOVA as supplementary (handles unbalanced designs) 7. Return FactorialAnovaResult dataclass
IMPORTANT: Use pingouin (already installed). Do NOT use import re to parse condition keys — use str.split("__") or similar.
task.acceptance_criteria: - eta-squared values in [0, 1] for all factors - omega-squared values in [-inf, 1] (can be negative for negligible effects) - Interaction term present in output - Synthetic data with known model effect produces significant F-test - Synthetic data with identical losses produces non-significant loss effect - condition_key parsing uses str.split, NOT regex - pingouin and statsmodels results are logged for cross-validation

task.name: Interaction plots (Model x Loss)
task.description:
Add _generate_interaction_plot() to biostatistics_figures.py.
Layout: 2-panel figure (left: clDice, right: MASD) X-axis: Loss function (3 levels) Y-axis: Mean metric value with 95% bootstrap CI Lines: One per model (4 lines), color-coded with Okabe-Ito palette Non-parallel lines = significant interaction (loss effect depends on model)
Also add _generate_variance_lollipop() for eta-squared visualization: Horizontal lollipop chart with factors on Y-axis and partial eta-squared on X-axis.

tdd_spec.tests: test_interaction_plot_generates_file test_interaction_plot_two_panels test_interaction_plot_sidecar_json test_interaction_plot_okabe_ito_colors test_variance_lollipop_generates_file test_variance_lollipop_all_factors test_variance_lollipop_sidecar_json

task.acceptance_criteria: - Interaction plot saved as PNG with JSON sidecar - Two panels (one per trusted metric) - 4 lines per panel (one per model) - Okabe-Ito colorblind-safe palette - Variance lollipop shows all 5 factors (Model, Loss, Interaction, Fold, Residual) - All figures have JSON sidecar with data for reproducibility

task.name: ANOVA summary LaTeX table
task.description:
Add _generate_anova_table() to biostatistics_tables.py.
Columns: Factor | df | SS | MS | F | p | eta_sq_partial | omega_sq Rows: Model, Loss, Model x Loss, Fold (random), Residual Separate table per metric (clDice, MASD, DSC)
Uses booktabs format (no vertical lines, three horizontal rules). Significance stars: * p<0.05, ** p<0.01, *** p<0.001
task.acceptance_criteria: - Valid LaTeX with booktabs - All factors present in rows - Significance stars match p-values - One table per metric

phase.name: Calibration Metrics
phase.description: Add modern calibration assessment metrics beyond ECE/MCE. These are evaluation-time metrics logged to MLflow, not training losses.

task.name: Calibration metrics: Brier, O:E, IPA, slope
task.description:
Add compute_calibration_metrics() returning: - Brier score (mean squared error of probabilities vs labels) - O:E ratio (observed/expected event rate per bin) - IPA (Integrated Probability Accuracy = 1 - Brier/Brier_null) - Calibration slope (logistic regression of outcome on log-odds)
These are voxel-level metrics for binary segmentation. For computational feasibility, subsample voxels (e.g., 100K random voxels per volume).
Use sklearn.metrics.brier_score_loss for Brier. Use sklearn.linear_model.LogisticRegression for slope. O:E = sum(y_true) / sum(p_pred) (with CI via bootstrap).

tdd_spec.tests: test_brier_score_perfect_predictions test_brier_score_random_predictions test_oe_ratio_calibrated test_oe_ratio_overconfident test_ipa_positive_for_good_model test_ipa_zero_for_null_model test_calibration_slope_perfect test_calibration_slope_overconfident test_calibration_metrics_voxel_subsampling

task.acceptance_criteria: - Brier score in [0, 1], 0 = perfect - O:E = 1.0 for calibrated predictions (within tolerance) - IPA in [-inf, 1], positive = better than null - Calibration slope = 1.0 for perfectly calibrated (within tolerance) - Voxel subsampling reduces compute without changing expected values - All metrics work with binary segmentation (foreground class)

task.name: Riley bootstrap instability plot
task.description:
Add compute_riley_instability() and _generate_instability_plot().
Bootstrap resampling of model rankings to assess stability: 1. Resample validation volumes with replacement (B=1000) 2. Re-rank all models on each bootstrap sample 3. Plot: X = bootstrap iteration, Y = rank, one line per model 4. Compute rank stability: fraction of bootstraps where model keeps its rank
This directly answers: "Would the model ranking change with a different validation set?" — critical for N=70 small-sample studies.
task.acceptance_criteria: - Bootstrap rank matrix shape: (n_bootstrap, n_models) - Deterministic with seed - Model with clearly best scores has high rank stability - Plot saved with JSON sidecar

phase.name: Cost Appendix and TRIPOD
phase.description: Add cost transparency artifacts for the manuscript appendix. Also add TRIPOD compliance verification test.

task.name: Cost appendix LaTeX table generation
task.description:
Add _generate_cost_appendix_table() to biostatistics_tables.py.
Reads cost/total_usd and cost/gpu_hourly_rate from MLflow runs. Generates a LaTeX table with: - Columns: Model | Loss | Aux Calib | Fold | GPU Hours | Cost ($) | Spot Savings - Summary row: Total cost, debug vs production breakdown - Footer: GPU type (L4), spot rate, region (europe-north1)
This addresses TRIPOD-LLM Item 12 (cost transparency) and Issue #795.
task.acceptance_criteria: - Valid LaTeX with booktabs - Total cost row sums individual costs - Spot savings computed as (on-demand - spot) / on-demand - Works with mock MLflow data for testing

task.name: TRIPOD compliance verification test
task.description:
Add a test that verifies the biostatistics flow output includes all TRIPOD+AI required artifacts. This is a meta-test that checks the lineage manifest against the TRIPOD compliance matrix.
Checks: - All P0 items from tripod-compliance-matrix.md are either present or explicitly marked as "manuscript-only" (not code-verifiable) - Statistical methods list includes all planned analyses - Figure catalog includes all planned figures - Table catalog includes all planned tables
task.acceptance_criteria: - Test verifies presence of ANOVA, Bayesian, Friedman, ICC methods - Test verifies interaction plot, specification curve, CD diagram in figures - Test verifies ANOVA table, cost table, ranking table in tables - Lineage manifest has all required fields

task.name: Integration test with synthetic factorial data
task.description:
End-to-end integration test that creates synthetic per-volume data matching the factorial design (4 models x 3 losses x 3 folds x 23 volumes) and runs the full biostatistics pipeline.
The synthetic data should have: - Known model effect (model A is better than model D) - Known loss effect (loss 2 is better than loss 1) - Known interaction (model B benefits more from loss 2 than model A) - Realistic metric ranges (clDice 0.5-0.95, MASD 0.1-5.0, DSC 0.6-0.9)
This test validates the entire pipeline from DuckDB construction through figure and table generation.
task.acceptance_criteria: - Full pipeline runs without error on synthetic data - DuckDB has correct table schema - ANOVA detects the planted model and loss effects - All figures and tables generated - Lineage manifest is complete - Test runs in <60 seconds (no GPU needed)
